item.title: The remarkable renaissance of high-frequency traders
item.description: Money, it’s a gas. Grab that cash with both hands and make a stash

item.title: War disruption forces world’s biggest condom maker to raise prices by up to 30%
item.description: Malaysia’s Karex says it has to pass on higher cost of raw materials to customers

item.title: FirstFT: Trump extends Iran ceasefire
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Deutsche Telekom weighs merger and Nestlé’s China business

item.title: Reebok plans hundreds of China stores to capture activewear boom
item.description: Owner Authentic Brands hopes bricks-and-mortar relaunch will generate $1bn in annual sales within a decade

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money podcast
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: The Swiss bank that crossed Scott Bessent
item.description: US pressure forced MBaer out of business, leaving critics questioning whether Swiss authorities had moved quickly enough

item.title: The pollution data they don’t print on your water bill
item.description: English water companies don’t publish numbers on how much waste they spill every year on your behalf — so I’ve estimated it

item.title: Trump trade chief urges US allies to pay more for critical minerals
item.description: Jamieson Greer says ‘security premium’ needed to counter reliance on Chinese supplies

item.title: New platform allows UK investors to hold crypto in Isas again
item.description: Start-up Stratiphy is the first to offer both crypto ETNs and Innovative Finance Isas

item.title: Is Norway really a war profiteer?
item.description: The country is facing pressure from its neighbours to share increased energy earnings to support Ukraine

item.title: Primark’s split is better fashionably late than never
item.description: Demerging retail creates two pure-play companies, both — given their scale — FTSE 100 constituents

item.title: OpenAI in talks to commit up to $1.5bn to private-equity joint venture
item.description: Start-up backing new company intended to help deploy AI within businesses owned by PE firms

item.title: Science in the shadow of the Third Reich
item.description: In postwar West Germany, the link between cigarettes and cancer was ignored — with real and deadly effects

item.title: How will new tenant laws affect me when I come to sell?
item.description: I’d like to sell my buy-to-let flat next year but worry about the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act

item.title: Europe is ceding sovereignty to SpaceX
item.description: The continent’s alternative is a jobs programme cosplaying as a space strategy

item.title: Insurers move to cap cyber payouts related to AI and ‘LLMjacking’
item.description: Beazley and QBE are among the groups proposing to limit losses from the rapidly advancing technology

item.title: Six lessons from history’s greatest financial crises
item.description: The Babylonians had debt defaults. The S&L scandal led to 2008. What else does the past tell us?

item.title: Pennsylvania’s chipmaking comeback left in limbo under Donald Trump
item.description: High-tech semiconductor manufacturing began in the Lehigh Valley but promised federal funds for its revival have not come through

item.title: US coal producers squeezed as Iran crisis lifts demand and costs
item.description: Soaring expense of production and transport hampers effort to boost exports

item.title: How Nestlé’s pioneering China business fell into disarray
item.description: Chinese distributors are chasing the Swiss giant for refunds after it allegedly oversupplied the market as demand dropped

item.title: UK pension funds face ‘huge’ costs to sell private assets
item.description: Industry regulator has written to 58 schemes to warn about their exposure to hard-to-sell investments

item.title: Military briefing: Trump bolsters Gulf force
item.description: A third US carrier group and up to 10,000 elite troops expected in region by end of month

item.title: How ancient Mesopotamians solved runaway debt
item.description: What we can – and can’t – learn from the practice of mass debt relief

item.title: They are history’s geniuses. But were they any good at investing?
item.description: How luminaries of the past managed their investment portfolios

item.title: Iran attacks container ship near Oman
item.description: Strike follows Tehran’s ambassador to UN accusing US of ‘wrongful acts’ in breach of ceasefire agreement

item.title: Prudential Financial extends Japan sales pause after mis-selling scandal
item.description: Insurer says changes will take longer to implement than had been expected

item.title: BHP strikes China iron ore deal after months of talks
item.description: Mining company firms up copper guidance in Mike Henry’s final results as chief executive

item.title: Can EVs kill off petrol cars in China?
item.description: Electric-vehicle makers target less affluent heartland for next phase of growth

item.title: SpaceX obtains right to buy AI start-up Cursor for $60bn
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket and AI conglomerate is seeking to catch up to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic

item.title: ‘Why isn’t the energy used by people?’: China’s global AI push hits resistance
item.description: TikTok’s plan for $9.5bn data centre on Brazil’s coast reflects national ambitions but is hit by environmental concerns
